summary: Fixed the QA-reported desktop horizontal overflow in the M3 appointment workspace. The M3 grid now uses shrinkable equal tracks, its summary/grid children opt into min-width: 0, long action buttons can wrap within their panel, and bulk rows no longer impose 120px minimum subcolumns. Added a Playwright 1440x1000 viewport measurement that asserts document scrollWidth does not exceed clientWidth and the M3 grid right edge stays within the viewport.

tests_run.result: Initial R2 run reproduced the remaining overflow after the first grid-track change: 3 tests passed, the new 1440px viewport check failed with scrollWidth 1451 > clientWidth 1440.
Chromium Playwright passed 4 tests, including the new M3 1440x1000 no-horizontal-overflow measurement.
Vite production web build passed; existing chunk-size warning for lib-a6cQy7l-.js over 500 kB remains.
Prettier check, ESLint, recursive typecheck, and architecture boundary check passed.
Task JSON validated successfully before R2 status update.

tests_run.command: node -e JSON parse/shape check for M3-CLIENT-APPOINTMENT-UI-001
tests_run.result: Parsed task and R2 handoff JSON; verified REVIEW status and qa_reviewer route.
Showed task implementation changes, the original writer handoff, the new R2 writer handoff, and lead/coordinator-owned goal/model/thread/route files already present in the worktree.

decisions: Fixed the overflow in CSS layout rather than hiding page overflow, so content remains visible and measurable.
Kept the test as a direct Playwright viewport measurement at 1440x1000 because it matches the QA repro and catches page-level horizontal overflow.
risks: The original task's live-query integration risk remains unchanged: this UI still uses protocol DTO projection fixtures until a future approved worker query adapter supplies the page-level appointment read model.
The Vite chunk-size warning remains unchanged and unrelated to the overflow fix.
requested_action: Review the R2 overflow fix and the new 1440x1000 Playwright measurement.
